violations.text: Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Inspector observed pasta placed hot in tightly covered plastic container. PIC removed cover to facilitate cooling and reviewed process with inspector. PIC discussed with staff. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/07/2025

Soups and sauces packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. PIC printed labels and placed on containers during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/07/2025

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (in contact with bottles) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Ice scoop was relocated during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/07/2025

Water was observed dripping in the basement under dish machine location. Per PIC, a pipe was damaged during a recent work; plumber is scheduled for service on 5/9/25. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2025

The door to the outside, located in the rear, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 06/08/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the wall in the warewashing area have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 06/08/2023

Pipe in basement was observed leaking into a bucket. Faucet at prep sink was observed leaking. Plumbing systems to be maintained in good repair and proper adjustment. New Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2023
